senses.gloss: to be full, to be satiated

examples.text: لَيْسَ الْمُؤْمِنُ الَّذِي يَشْبَعُ وَجَارُهُ جَائِعٌ
examples.english: He is not a believer who eats his fill while his neighbor goes hungry.

senses.gloss: to be fed up, to have had it

etymology.text:
Compare Hebrew שָׂבַע (savá', “to be sated, to be satisfied”) and Assyrian Neo-Aramaic ܣܵܒ݂ܹܥ (sāḇēˁ, “to be satiated from food”).

Verbal noun of شَبِعَ (šabiʕa).
